name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BYPASS 1/21
units: metres below ground level; depth = base of interval. Reduced Level (OD Level mOD) column cross-checked (90.50 - depth).

intervals.verbatim: Mottled orange brown and grey sandy silty CLAY with occasional fine and medium gravel
Stiff and very stiff orange brown and brown with occasional grey mottles sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular and subangular gravel and occasional gravel of coal
annotations: coal present only as reworked gravel clasts, not a seam

intervals.verbatim: Loose to medium dense and medium dense orange brown clayey sandy SILT with occasional black micaceous pockets, occasional gravel of coal and some pockets of brown clayey slightly sandy silt
Medium dense orange brown clayey silty fine and medium SAND with occasional black micaceous pockets, occasional gravel of coal and pockets of brown clayey silty fine sand
annotations: unconsolidated SAND (drift), not sandstone; spans sheet 1->2 (sheet 2 says 'See previous sheet')

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense laminated brown clayey sandy SILT with occasional reddish brown silty clay laminations, occasional fine and medium angular gravel and fine and medium sand pockets

Medium dense brown clayey silty fine SAND with occasional bands of brown sandy silty clay, occasional laminations of grey silty clay, occasional pockets of black micaceous fine and medium sand and occasional gravel of coal
Firm greyish brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to rounded gravel
annotations: with water: GWe marked at 26.00m; Remarks: groundwater encountered at 26.00m, level rose 0.20m in 30 minutes

intervals.verbatim: Laminated brownish grey silty CLAY with occasional coarse subrounded gravel and occasional silt partings
annotations: with water: GWc marked at 27.00m; Remarks: seepage cut-off by casing at 27.00m

intervals.verbatim: Stiff greyish brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine and medium angular gravel and occasional coarse angular to subrounded gravel and cobbles with some bands of laminated reddish brown silty clay with occasional fine and medium angular gravel
annotations: sheet 4 restates a fresh description at 30.00m (no 'See previous sheet'), so 30.00 read as a bed boundary

intervals.verbatim: Firm and stiff grey sandy silty CLAY with some fine and medium angular to subrounded gravel and occasional coarse angular to subrounded gravel and cobbles: very sandy in places. Some clayey silty fine and medium sand pockets
Dense brown clayey silty SAND with occasional pockets of black micaceous fine and medium sand and occasional medium subrounded gravel
Dense and very dense brown clayey silty fine SAND and occasional gravel of coal
annotations: with water: GWe marked at 36.50m; Remarks: second groundwater seepage encountered at 36.50m, level rose 1.40m in 30 minutes

intervals.verbatim: Stiff grey very sandy silty CLAY with some fine and medium angular to subrounded gravel
Grey silty fine SAND with bands of medium sand
Laminated brown clayey sandy SILT with occasional fine angular gravel

notes: Modern typed metric log (Wimpey Geotech / Roughton & Partners), 'A167 Durham Western Bypass, Supplementary Ground Investigation', BOREHOLE 1/21, cable percussion (Pilcon Wayfarer), commenced 29/5/92. 8 page images: sheets 1-4 of 4 are the strata log (0.00-38.70m, 16 beds); pages 5-8 are site plans / OS location maps with no strata (Site Plan (1), (2), (3) + a general layout) and are ignored for the bed model. Entire hole is superficial DRIFT (glacial/glaciofluvial clays, silts, sands with gravel and cobbles); no bedrock reached, no coal seams. 'occasional gravel of coal' in several beds is reworked coal detritus, not an in-situ seam. SAND/SILT/CLAY here are unconsolidated drift, not sandstone/siltstone/mudstone bedrock. All 16 OD reduced-level cross-checks agree exactly with 90.50-depth; every thickness+depth chain is internally consistent, no clerk arithmetic slips. Groundwater: first strike GWe 26.00m (rose 0.20m/30min), cut off by casing GWc 27.00m; second seepage GWe 36.50m (rose 1.40m/30min). Inspection pit hand-dug to 1.20m. Lockable protective steel cover concreted over a piezometer installation. Log fully legible.
